pattern: Doubling only adds factors of two, so a subarray's gcd can grow at most once and only by paying to double every element tied for the fewest twos.
hints: Multiplying by two can never introduce new odd factors, so the gcd's odd part is fixed and only its power of two can change.
The power of two in the gcd equals the smallest count of twos among the elements; raising it means doubling every element that currently has that smallest count.
For each subarray track its gcd and how many elements share the minimum number of trailing twos; if that many doublings fit in k you may double the gcd, otherwise the score is just length times gcd.

edge_inputs.description: doubling all minimum-two elements raises gcd
too few operations to lift the gcd of the long subarray

stress.description: small arrays of small values so the subset-brute oracle stays fast
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 7))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 40) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
